title: linaria aeruginea subsp. atrofusca
intro: Linaria aeruginea subsp. atrofusca, also known as the Purple Toadflax, is a plant species belonging to the borage family (Boraginaceae). Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, including its expectorant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The plant's health benefits include its ability to soothe sore throats and ease congestion, making it a popular remedy in herbal medicine. Linaria aeruginea subsp. atrofusca can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or syrup, allowing for various herbal preparations to be used depending on the desired effect.
